note: 8 new leads across 4 pipelines (pr-marketing, fund-formation, legal-consulting, ai-consulting). Fund Formation is the hottest category again — 3 fresh emerging-manager / new-platform launches this week (Aqualis Partners debut SPV, Sycamore Tree Capital credit secondaries platform, 5c(c) Capital prediction-market VC fund). Two warm AI-consulting targets freshly funded: Gumloop ($50M Series B from Benchmark, March 12) and InsightFinder ($15M Series B from Yu Galaxy, April 16 — explicitly hiring first sales + marketing team). Checkbox ($23M Series A, AI Legal Front Door) and Felix (ex-Parrot founder, $1.7M pre-seed April 7) round out a strong legal-consulting pair. Shepherd's $42M Series B on March 24 = freshest post-raise PR amplification trigger on the board. Neon DB unreachable from sandbox; run `node scripts/import-2026-04-18.cjs` locally to import. Deduplicated against 52 existing companies from prior scrapes (Wealth.com, Summize, Dolomite, Eyre Street, Air Street, Fedaia, Ferghana, OpenArc, Orbital, depthfirst, Tazapay, Round Treasury, and ~40 others already in DB).

leads.name: Shepherd
leads.size: 600+ customers, 1,500+ policies, $400B+ project value insured (7x revenue growth in 24 months)
leads.source: PR Newswire / Carrier Management / FinTech Global / Insurance-Edge (March 24-26, 2026)
leads.notes: Closed $42M Series B on March 24, 2026 led by Intact Private Capital (insurance giant; also carrier partner), with Spark Capital and Costanoa. Tagline 'insurance behind the AI infrastructure boom' — positioning themselves at the intersection of AI + construction + data center buildout. 7x revenue growth in 24 months, insuring hyperscalers, chipmakers, general contractors building AI facilities. Classic freshly-raised B2B fintech = premium post-raise PR amplification target. CEO/founder is public-facing with substantive blog content already — easy to scale. Ties into AI infrastructure narrative which is the hottest PR vertical of 2026.

leads.name: InsightFinder
leads.size: Sub-30 person team, revenue 3x YoY; customers include UBS, NBCUniversal, Lenovo, Dell, Google Cloud, Comcast
leads.location: Research Triangle, NC (NC State academic roots)
leads.fundingStage: Series B ($15M, April 16, 2026) — total raised $35M, led by Yu Galaxy
leads.notes: Raised $15M Series B from Yu Galaxy on April 16, 2026 (2 days ago) — freshest trigger on the board. 15 years of academic research at NC State; launched Autonomous Reliability Insights (ARI) product. Tier-1 customer logos (UBS, NBC, Google Cloud, Dell, Comcast). CEO explicitly told press the capital will 'fuel first dedicated sales and marketing hires.' That is a textbook AI-consulting signal: they are about to hire, need positioning/messaging help, and have no marketing DNA yet. Academic founder (NC State professor) suggests coachability. Strong trigger + strong pain + fresh money.

contacts.name: Gelin Gu

leads.industry: AI Agent Platform / Enterprise Workflow Automation
leads.size: Growth stage; customers include Shopify, Ramp, Gusto, Samsara, Instacart, Opendoor
leads.fundingStage: Series B ($50M, March 12, 2026) — led by Benchmark (Everett Randle's first investment at the firm)
leads.source: TechCrunch / Business Wire / Gumloop Blog / TechFundingNews (March 12, 2026)
leads.notes: Raised $50M Series B led by Benchmark on March 12, 2026 with Nexus VP, First Round, YC, Box Group, Shopify Ventures. Founder Max Brodeur-Urbas has said he's now 'building a dedicated sales force' — implies marketing/positioning need as well. Adjacent to Katie's AI-consulting ICP: enterprise AI agent deployment is the #1 buyer question today, and Gumloop is arguably a consulting partner more than a competitor. Potential both as a direct pr-marketing lead and as a channel partner for the AI-consulting practice.

leads.name: Checkbox
leads.industry: LegalTech / AI Legal Intake & Workflow Orchestration
leads.size: 100+ enterprise customers (SAP, PepsiCo, Hitachi, multiple Fortune 500)
leads.fundingStage: Series A ($23M, January 28, 2026) at $100M valuation — led by Touring Capital; Peak XV, Conductive, Tidal, Five V; angel: Jerry Ting (ex-Evisort CEO)
leads.source: Law.com / Artificial Lawyer / Axios / Business Wire (January 28, 2026)
leads.notes: Global 'AI Legal Front Door' for in-house legal teams — 83% of routine legal/compliance requests at Hitachi are now partially or fully automated by Checkbox. Jerry Ting (ex-Evisort CEO, now Workday VP/Head of Agentic AI) invested in the round — strong industry validation. Selling into enterprise in-house legal = high adjacency with our legal-consulting pipeline for Fortune 500 deployment advisory, change management, and compliance-driven rollouts. Less 'we need a lawyer' and more 'we want a strategic partner as we scale into the enterprise' — angle is advisory/ops, not transactional legal work.

leads.name: Felix
leads.industry: LegalTech / AI Hyperautomation for Professional Services
leads.fundingStage: Pre-seed ($1.7M, April 7, 2026) — led by XYZ Venture Capital
leads.notes: Launched publicly on April 7, 2026 with $1.7M pre-seed led by XYZ Venture Capital. CEO Tomas Scavnicky previously built Parrot (legal workflow automation — acquired by FileVine in 2025). Angel participation from leaders at Amazon, Apple, Palantir, Flexport, Yelp, Midjourney. Selling into legal, finance, insurance — three of Katie's pipelines directly touch. Fresh launch + pre-seed = founder in full GTM mode, very coachable, needs positioning work. Small budget now but fast trajectory (serial founder w/ successful exit).

leads.name: Aqualis Partners
leads.industry: Private Equity Secondaries (LP-led, GP-led, directs, preferreds)
leads.size: New firm — debut SPV in market $100M–$200M; Fund I targeting $400M–$750M later in 2026
leads.fundingStage: Launched Oct 2025; debut SPV raising now; Fund I H2 2026
leads.source: PR Newswire / SecondariesInvestor / PitchBook / Private Equity International (March-April 2026)
leads.notes: Founder Cari Lodge is a 25-year secondaries veteran — previously led CF Private Equity secondaries (Commonfund subsidiary). Named one of Private Equity News' 'Twenty Most Influential in Secondaries 2025.' New GP, debut SPV now, Fund I landing later this year = perfect fund-formation ICP with a long runway of legal/fund-structure work ahead: LPA + side letters + GP restructures for both SPV and Fund I, plus ongoing opportunistic deal-by-deal work. Picking strength: brand-name founder, clean spinout, institutional LP base likely.

leads.name: Sycamore Tree Capital Partners
leads.size: ~33 professionals; Dallas HQ + NY office; new platform launching
leads.fundingStage: Credit secondaries investment platform launched April 13, 2026; new strategy hires underway
leads.source: Alternative Credit Investor / Morningstar / Business Wire / Alternatives Watch (April 13-14, 2026)
leads.notes: Founded by Mark Okada (Highland Capital co-founder), Trey Parker, Jack Yang. On April 13, 2026 announced a dedicated credit secondaries investment platform led by new hire Robert O'Connor as partner/PM. New strategy inside an existing firm = new LPA, new fund docs, new GP entity, new vehicle structures — meaningful fund-formation work even if not a 'Fund I emerging manager.' Institutional sophistication = likely paying for top-tier counsel, which is either risk (locked in) or opportunity (room for specialized secondaries-counsel angle).

leads.name: 5c(c) Capital
leads.industry: Venture Capital (Prediction Markets specialist — first fund in the category)
leads.fundingStage: Fund I raising, announced March 23, 2026; ~20 portfolio companies over 2 years; already backed by marquee investors
leads.source: Fortune / Bloomberg / Coindesk / The Block / Crowdfund Insider (March 23, 2026)
leads.notes: Debut VC fund from two early Kalshi employees (Noah Zingler-Sternig ex-Head of Ops; Adhi Rajaprabhakaran ex-Kalshi market-maker trader) — category-defining as the FIRST prediction-markets-dedicated VC fund. Named for CEA §5c(c), the prediction markets exchange section. Backers: Shayne Coplan (Polymarket CEO), Tarek Mansour (Kalshi CEO), Marc Andreessen, Micky Malka (Ribbit), Kyle Samani (ex-Multicoin). Operators going GP for the first time = heavy legal need (LPA, fund docs, CFTC/SEC nuances given the prediction markets regulatory overlay). Strong ICP fit for fund-formation with a regulatory-adjacent angle. Website still developing — outreach is via LinkedIn/personal networks.
